content: Just as language learners build long-term retention of new vocabulary (like the term "reflexion" itself) through periodic explicit reflection on usage and meaning in high school English workshops, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by integrating spaced, structured vocabulary-level reflection rather than only reflecting on full outputs. This draws on the human learning principle that anchoring reflection to discrete core units improves error correction more effectively than open-ended post-hoc reflection.
